rulings.Case Title: PATRINA HARRISON VS. JPMORGAN CHASE BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 1. DEFENDANT JP MORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs. The motion for attorney fees and costs is denied without prejudice because it lacks clarity ("all other applicable laws"). (See id. at 2:1, CRC 3.1112(d)(3).) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: EASTON RUHNKE ET AL VS. HUBBELL POWER SYSTEMS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 11. DEFENDANT QUANTA ENERGY SERVICES, LLC'S Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. "Quanta Energy Services' motion for determination of good faith settlement" is denied without prejudice. Key factors in an analysis under Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (1985) 38 Cal.3d 488 are "a rough approximation of plaintiffs' total recovery and the settlor's proportionate liability" and "the financial conditions and insurance policy limits of settling defendants." (Id. at 499.) However, beyond a little unsupported and imprecise hyperbole, this crucial information has not been provided to the court. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. rulings.Case Title: RONALD DAVID RIVERS VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 2. DEFENDANTS ELISA STEPHENS, SUE ROWLEY, REID RAUKAR, MARGUERITE CROOKS, MICHAEL VARTAIN, STEPHENS INSTITUTE'S Petition To Confirm Arbitration Award. Defendants' petition to confirm the arbitration award is granted. Plaintiff fails to establish one of the limited, enumerated grounds to vacate or correct the award. (CCP 1286.2; CCP 1286.6.) The court already determined that the parties had an enforceable arbitration agreement and there is no basis to revisit that ruling. rulings.Case Title: RONALD DAVID RIVERS VS. ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities, Declaration
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 2. PLAINTIFF RONALD RIVERS'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is off calendar. The motion violates the court's order staying this case. The motion is also procedurally defective. Plaintiff seeks summary judgment on his complaint, but does not present evidence to support such an order. The parties resolved this dispute through arbitration. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINA M. SAGONOWSKY VS. JONATHAN WETZEL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Rick Belgarde To Comply With Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Rick Belgarde; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof; Declaration Of Bradley R. White
rulings.Rulings: Set for Discovery on Thursday, December 21, 2023 Line 1, PLAINTIFF CHRISTINA SAGONOWSKY's Motion To Compel Rick Belgarde To Comply With Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Rick Belgarde. Pro Tem Judge John-Paul Deol, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The unopposed motion is granted. For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPARTMENT 301 DAILY AT 9:00 A.M.), or dial the corresponding number and use the meeting ID, and password for Department 301. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to JPDeol@dhillonlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN REINER VS. TAYUAN MA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 663, As Well As Code Of Civil Procedure Section 473(D), To Set Aside And Vacate The September 27, 2023 Order Sustaining The Demurrer To The Complaint Of Defendants Tayuan Ma And Tao Zhang
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 12. Plaintiff's "motion pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 663, as well as Code of Civil Procedure section 473(d), to set aside and vacate the September 27, 2023 order sustaining the demurrer to the complaint of defendants Tayuan Ma and Tao Zhang" is denied. CCP 663 regards judgments, not demurrer rulings. (Id.; Payne v. Rader (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1569, 1574-75.) CCP 473(d) regards "clerical mistakes" in orders (none of which is identified here), not alleged legal errors. (Id.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SKYLINE CAPITAL BUILDERS LLC, VS. SFII SHARON, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 7. PLAINTIFF SKYLINE CAPITAL BUILDERS LLC'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS. Plaintiff's motion to deem cases CGC-23-606211, CGC-23-607161, CGC-23-607203, CGC-23-607206, CGC-23-607223, CGC-23-607439, CGC-23-607791, CGC-23-607823, CGC-23-608019, and CGC-23-609034 related is granted. (CRC 3.300(h).) The parties may seek a single assignment from the Presiding Judge in department 206. The motion to consolidate is denied. While there is overlap among the cases, the subcontractor actions have simple and discrete issues. Consolidation will cause prejudice, delay, and create manageability issues. The parties are ordered to coordinate discovery to reduce duplication of effort. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JONATHAN BIKOFF VS. LIONTREE, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, December 21, 2023, line 10. DEFENDANTS NICHOLAS TUOSTO, BEN BRAUN, LIONTREE, LLC'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT. Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is overruled. Plaintiff pleads an unpaid employee bonus and vacation time in causes of action for (1) unpaid wages and penalties, (2) unpaid vacation and (3) unfair competition. Defendants argue that plaintiff's view of the bonus is contrary to an "offer letter" referred to in the complaint, and thus the first and third counts are barred. However, the offer letter is not attached to the complaint as an exhibit, nor does the complaint plead all the offer's principal terms. Thus, defendants' demurrer improperly relies on unpled extrinsic matter. (Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) A footnote in Filet Menu, Inc. v. Cheng (1999) 17 Cal.App.4th 1276, 1278 is not to the contrary; there, the complaint incorporated a contract by reference, which is not so here. (Id. at 2:18-23.) Further, even were the offer letter incorporated by reference, while defendants say the bonus was discretionary per the offer, the complaint pleads that it was not. Given such ambiguity, plaintiff's interpretation must be accepted as correct in testing the complaint's sufficiency. (Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239.) Finally, the complaint adequately pleads claims against the two individual defendants. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
